Suppose a large ship is sinking in the middle of the ocean. There is only one lifeboat, and it can only hold ten people, but there are eleven people on the ship

Ten of the passengers are healthy, productive members of society. The eleventh person is a world-renowned doctor who is currently unconscious and requires immediate medical attention to survive, but they possess the knowledge to cure a disease currently affecting thousands

**If you are the person in charge of the lifeboat, do you leave the unconscious doctor behind to ensure the safety of the ten healthy passengers, or do you remove one healthy person to room for the doctor? What is the primary ethical principle guiding your choice?**
